items.title: Machine Learning - Wikipedia
items.summary:
Machine learning is a branch of artificial intelligence and computer science that focuses on the development of algorithms and statistical models that enable computer systems to learn and improve from experience without being explicitly programmed. The field encompasses various approaches to teaching machines to recognize patterns, make decisions, and solve problems by analyzing data.

Machine learning is fundamentally different from traditional programming in that instead of writing explicit instructions for every possible scenario, developers create systems that can learn from examples and data. This approach has become increasingly important as the volume of data generated by modern systems has grown exponentially.

The field is closely related to statistics, mathematics, and data science, utilizing techniques from these domains to process and analyze large datasets. Machine learning applications span numerous industries and domains, from healthcare and finance to entertainment and autonomous systems.

The discipline intersects with cybernetics and broader learning theory, representing a significant evolution in how computers are programmed and how they process information. Machine learning continues to be an active area of research and development, with ongoing work to improve algorithm efficiency, accuracy, and applicability across different problem domains.
